outcome: `BANDIT-055` landed and closed out the bootstrap-gap chore for `BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE`. The work adds a repo-native Token-Cost Failsafe policy artifact, validation command, work-item creation integration, generated brief rendering, provider-pricing evidence requirements, benchmark/evaluation spend boundaries, recurring paid-route promotion prerequisites, soft budget bands, continuation-decision evidence, and trace-cost non-authority rules. Stage 4 CodeRabbit produced actionable findings that were repaired or dispositioned, then the final focused refresh timed out twice and is recorded as provider-refusal/bootstrap_gap replacement evidence rather than pass evidence. Refreshed Local Qwen returned `non_blocking` findings that Codex PM dispositioned for explicit Stage 6 no-action routing. Stage 5 landing verdict and local-record landing action evidence are recorded, explicit layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence are recorded, and no Evidence SLO policy, scheduler execution, worktree lifecycle work, claim lease creation or release, work-surface reservation implementation, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, PR/CI workflow, paid reviewer promotion, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work was introduced.
what_worked: The chore converted the accepted token-cost failsafe boundary into repo-native policy, validation, work-item spec requirements, and generated brief evidence without promoting any paid reviewer, paid model, scheduler, or long-running route.

Focused RED and implementation evidence covered missing policy validation, provider-pricing evidence, benchmark/evaluation spend, recurring paid routes, soft budget bands, continuation decisions, trace-cost non-authority, and generated brief integration.

Stage 3 implementation preserved the Bootstrap Model-Family Separation and Permanent Test Ownership Boundary while keeping tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, and acceptance mappings out of Writer authority.

Stage 4 evidence stayed honest: CodeRabbit timeout evidence is dispositioned as provider-refusal/bootstrap_gap replacement evidence, Local Qwen findings are accepted as non-blocking, and aggregate review evidence records the current review-subject hash.

Stage 5 landing used current review-subject hash evidence, layered risk-classification evidence, supply-chain gate evidence, land-check, auto-land-check, and local-record landing action without expanding workflow authority.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Token-cost controls should be generous abnormal-run failsafes backed by explicit provider-pricing and spend evidence, not tight caps that force duplicate failed attempts.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `BANDIT-055` adds Token-Cost Failsafe policy validation, CLI validation, work-item creation spec requirements, generated brief rendering, focused tests, implementation evidence, review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 closeout; `.bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json` marks `BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE` resolved during closeout.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: PM-owned Stage 4 repair tests do not invalidate the Stage 3 Claude Writer Test Ownership Boundary.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: Git evidence and `docs/work/BANDIT-055/qwen-finding-disposition.md` show that the Stage 3 Claude Writer commit did not edit tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, or acceptance mappings. Later test additions were Codex PM-owned Stage 4 repair/regression evidence, so no source repair or standalone chore is required.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: `locally_resolved_pending_refresh` is a bounded CodeRabbit timeout-disposition state, not a general pass path.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `docs/work/BANDIT-055/coderabbit-timeout-disposition.md`, `docs/work/BANDIT-055/coderabbit-review.md`, and `docs/work/BANDIT-055/qwen-finding-disposition.md` record that `locally_resolved_pending_refresh` remains fail-closed unless explicit provider-refusal/bootstrap_gap replacement evidence exists. CodeRabbit is not treated as pass evidence.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Missing Token-Cost Failsafe policy remains bootstrap-compatible while malformed present policy fails closed.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: The validator intentionally allows repositories without a token-cost policy before the gap is active, but fails closed on malformed present policy and on missing required paid-route, pricing, spend, continuation, or trace-cost boundary fields. No additional documentation chore is required before the next gap.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Artifact-specific Evidence SLOs remain the next trust-signal gap after token-cost failsafe closeout.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `BANDIT-GAP-EVIDENCE-FRESHNESS-SLOS` remains queued for artifact-specific freshness budgets, Evidence Trust Signals, staleness reasons, and cockpit/view-model validation. `BANDIT-055` deliberately did not implement Evidence SLO policy.

structured_improvement_mining.finding: No required Stage 6 tool call failed during closeout. Earlier CodeRabbit final refresh attempts timed out twice after prior actionable findings were repaired or dispositioned, and those timeouts were already recorded as provider-refusal/bootstrap_gap replacement evidence before this closeout.
structured_improvement_mining.disposition: explicit no-action decision - no new bootstrap gap or follow-up is needed for Stage 6 failed tool calls

structured_improvement_mining.finding: The work stayed bounded to Token-Cost Failsafe policy validation, provider-pricing evidence, spend boundaries, soft budget bands, continuation decisions, trace-cost non-authority, review/landing evidence, retrospective closeout, bootstrap-gap disposition, and roadmap/current-context synchronization.
structured_improvement_mining.disposition: explicit no-action decision - no Evidence SLO policy, scheduler execution, claim/worktree authority, cockpit UI/server/API, PR/CI workflow, merge, push, deploy, product UAT, paid-reviewer promotion, dependency, lockfile, installed-skill, or unrelated Phase 8 work started

structured_improvement_mining.finding: The gap was well-sized as one bootstrap chore: define and validate abnormal-run token-cost failsafes and paid/high-token execution evidence before the queued Evidence Freshness SLO gap.
structured_improvement_mining.disposition: explicit no-action decision - Evidence Freshness SLOs remain a separate queued gap

structured_improvement_mining.finding: The established invocation paths for pre-PR CodeRabbit, Local Qwen, review-subject hash, token-cost-failsafe validation, risk classification, supply-chain gate, land-check, auto-land-check, local landing, artifact creation, validation, gaps list, cockpit status, session context, and diff hygiene were available. The only material friction was CodeRabbit provider timeout, which is already dispositioned.

structured_improvement_mining.finding: The final CodeRabbit timeout sequence required explicit replacement evidence, but the token-cost validator itself should reduce future abnormal-run repetition by forcing continuation decisions, retry triggers, and cost/pricing evidence into policy before paid or high-token routes become available.

structured_improvement_mining.finding: No paid reviewer call, recurring paid model route, spend-class approval, benchmark spend, live external service, dependency install, or live SCA provider was introduced. The implemented policy defines the evidence required before future paid or high-token routes are treated as available.

structured_improvement_mining.finding: No uncertainty remains for local Token-Cost Failsafe policy validation and work-item integration after focused tests, implementation evidence, review evidence, PM finding disposition, landing verdict, local-record landing action, and closeout. Future uncertainty remains in Evidence Freshness SLOs and later cockpit/product work.
structured_improvement_mining.disposition: queued bootstrap gap - BANDIT-GAP-EVIDENCE-FRESHNESS-SLOS remains the next queued gap

improvement_chores:
No new immediate retrospective-derived improvement chore is created by this closeout.

The accepted Local Qwen findings are dispositioned as explicit no-action decisions in this retrospective and in `docs/work/BANDIT-055/qwen-finding-disposition.md`: PM-owned Stage 4 repair tests did not invalidate the Stage 3 Writer Test Ownership Boundary, `locally_resolved_pending_refresh` is bounded to CodeRabbit timeout disposition and remains fail-closed without replacement evidence, and missing-policy bootstrap compatibility is distinct from malformed-policy fail-closed validation.

`BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE` is resolved by `BANDIT-055`. The next queued bootstrap gap is `BANDIT-GAP-EVIDENCE-FRESHNESS-SLOS`, which should become `BANDIT-056` before unrelated Phase 8 work. Evidence SLO policy, full scheduler execution, full worktree lifecycle implementation, cockpit UI/server/API work, PR/CI workflow, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT scope, paid reviewer promotion, dependency or lockfile changes, installed global skill edits, external service integration, or unrelated Phase 8 work remains blocked until queued bootstrap gaps are resolved or explicitly dispositioned.
cross_model_tension: No unresolved cross-model tension remains for `BANDIT-055`. CodeRabbit produced actionable findings that were repaired or dispositioned, then the final two focused refresh attempts timed out and are recorded as provider-refusal/bootstrap_gap replacement evidence without claiming CodeRabbit pass. Local Qwen returned non-blocking tracking findings that Codex PM accepted and dispositioned with explicit no-action decisions. Codex PM accepted Stage 4 landability without requiring escalated review.
bootstrap_gaps_remaining: `BANDIT-GAP-EVIDENCE-FRESHNESS-SLOS` is open and queued as the next bootstrap-gap chore.
Unrelated Phase 8 cockpit work remains blocked while any open bootstrap gap remains queued or active.
